Package i5.cb.telos

Class Transform

  • java.lang.Object
    • i5.cb.telos.Transform

  • public class Transform
extends java.lang.Object
    Transformations between Frame and Proposition representation

      • toObjectName

        public static ObjectName toObjectName​(TelosObject to)
        transform a Telos object into an objectname
        Parameters:
        to - the object to transform
        Returns:
        the objectname that uniquely identifies to -postcondition result != null

      • toObjectNames

        public static ObjectNames toObjectNames​(ITelosObjectSet tos,
                                        boolean bIncludeSystemClasses)
        transform a set of Telos objects into a set of objectnames
        Parameters:
        tos - the objects to transform
        Returns:
        the objectnames that uniquely identify tos' elements -postcondition result != null

      • toObjectNamedProposition

        public static java.lang.String toObjectNamedProposition​(TelosObject to)
        Returns:
        a proposition-like representation, where source and destination are displayed as object names rather than OIDs

      • toObjectNamedPropositions

        public static java.lang.String toObjectNamedPropositions​(ITelosObjectSet tos)
        Returns:
        a proposition-like representation, where source and destination are displayed as object names rather than OIDs

      • toTelosObject

        public static TelosObject toTelosObject​(ObjectName objectName,
                                        ITelosObjectSet tos)
                                 throws CBException
        transforms objectName into a Telos object. If the object or dependant objects (source and destination) don't exist, they are created on the fly. No objects are inserted into tos, though.
        Parameters:
        objectName - the object name to transform
        tos - != null: try to refer dependant objects (source and destination) in tos. Attributes can be uniquely identified by object name within a single extension, but not within a JVM. Thus, if tos != null, we always try to refer an existing attribute in tos. If tos doesn't contain such an attribute, we create it on-the-fly (but don't insert it). As we don't know its destination, we use null as destination.
        Returns:
        the Telos object that corresponds to objectName
        Throws:
        CBException - if the transformation is not possible. This may only happen if the object name is not well-formed.

      • toTelosObjectSet

        public static ITelosObjectSet toTelosObjectSet​(ObjectNames objectNames)
                                        throws CBException
        transforms objectNames into Telos objects.
        Parameters:
        objectNames - the object names to transform
        Returns:
        the Telos object set that corresponds to objectNames
        Throws:
        CBException - if the transformation is not possible. This may happen if the object name contains an attribute which is not contained in tos, or the object name consists of something other than labels and select expressions.

      • toTelosObjectSet

        public static ITelosObjectSet toTelosObjectSet​(AttrCategories categories,
                                               ITelosObjectSet tos,
                                               ITelosObjectSet tosClasses)
                                        throws CBException
        same as toTelosObjectSet( ObjectNames )
        Throws:
        CBException

        Returns:
        tf as Telos object set

      • toTelosFrames

        public static TelosFrames toTelosFrames​(ITelosObjectSet tos,
                                        TelosObject toClass)
        Parameters:
        toClass - "starting point"
        Returns:
        tos as TelosFrames

      • toTelosFrames

        public static TelosFrames toTelosFrames​(ITelosObjectSet tos)
        Returns:
        tos as TelosFrames

      • toFrameString

        public static java.lang.String toFrameString​(java.util.List telosObjects,
                                             boolean bRecursive)
        Convert a list of Telos Objects to string of Telos frames. The argument bRecursive should be true if the result is used in TELL, and should be false if the result is used in UNTELL. If bRecursive is true, also the source and destination objects are represented as frames, so that the referential integrity is correct.

      • toFrameString

        public static java.lang.String toFrameString​(TelosObject to,
                                             boolean bRecursive)
        Convert a Telos object to a Telos frame string
